steps.title: Add the equations to eliminate y
steps.content:
The first equation has $5y$ and the second has $-5y$. Adding them will cancel the $y$ terms.
$$5y + (-5y) = (6x + 17) + (7x - 23)$$
$$0 = 13x - 6$$
This simplifies to $13x = 6$.

steps.title: Find 39x using the relationship between 39 and 13
steps.content:
Notice that $39 = 3 \times 13$, so we can express $39x$ in terms of $13x$:
$$39x = 3(13x) = 3(6) = 18$$
That matches choice D — D is correct.
